label: Wide-shell 2BR / 1 bath, LD style — side-split with HALL in middle band; public column is a separate living/dining/kitchen stacked front-to-rear (not a combined great room). Bedrooms stack front-to-rear on the LEFT (master front, standard rear); hall sits in the middle band south of common bath; both bedrooms and the common bath door into the hall; hall opens east into DINING (the middle-row public room).

adjacencies.note: master (front) and standard (rear) stack vertically in the left column — shared horizontal wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
master's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — master's primary public access; the hall buffers master from the public side. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
standard's south wall opens into the hall — standard's only public access. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
standard's east wall touches common's west wall at the rear — shared wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
common bath's south wall opens south into the hall — the single bath's only door; both bedrooms reach it via the hall. DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
common bath shares plumbing wall with kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'common_access' group. Unchanged from the gr sibling.

hall's east wall is an open mouth into dining, not living — the hall sits in the MIDDLE band, so its wall aligns with whichever public room occupies that same middle row of the living/dining/kitchen stack (dining). Takes over the role hall<->great played in the gr sibling. 0.9 m mouth = PH single-person minimum on the compact envelope.
living and dining stack front-to-rear — open mouth, no wall (ld style).
dining and kitchen stack front-to-rear — open pass-through, matches the solver's default kitchen+dining-must-stack rule directly.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_w_hall_ld_single_bath
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Unchanged from the gr sibling — the ld conversion solved 0-warning across the whole feasible band with these values, so no retuning was needed. kitchen depth floors at 1.8 m galley clearance; hall depth at 1.0 m PH minimum corridor; common bath 3.0-4.5 m2; standard capped at 10 m2 so it doesn't steal from the LDK stack.

notes: Created 2026-07-21 as an LDK split (living/dining/kitchen stacked
front-to-rear, the solver's default LDK arrangement) of the combined-
great-room 1s_2br_wd_side_split_bath_hall_gr. This is a SIBLING, not a
replacement: the gr version is kept (it is the fallback target for three
other wide topologies AND it reaches shallower lots this ld version
can't — see the depth floor below).
Only ONE adjacency changed role: hall's open-mouth connection moves from
great_room to dining. The hall sits in the middle band, so its wall has
to align with the middle-row public room (dining, between living-front
and kitchen-rear). living gets hosts_entry instead of great (front-most
public room, same role). Everything else (private column, hall structure,
single common bath, wet-stack, lot_adjustment_profile) is unchanged.
Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'):
Rear band:    [STANDARD       | COMMON T&B | KITCHEN          ]
Middle band:  [(master cont.) | HALL       | DINING           ]
Front band:   [MASTER BEDROOM             | LIVING           ]
FEASIBILITY — a broad, well-behaved 0-warning band, notably better than
the clustered-bath wide ld sibling (1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_cl_ld,
which is narrow-band). Reason: single-bath, no ensuite / no clustered
bath block / no match_bedroom_widths / no match_bath_widths, so the
private side has far fewer competing width constraints and master's
full-width front band absorbs slack. Verified feasible (0 warnings)
across buildable width 7-12 m and buildable depth >= 6 m — lots roughly
11x10 up through 16x12.
DEPTH FLOOR = buildable depth 6 m (lot depth 10). Below that the
living/dining/kitchen 3-room stack can't fit (14x9.5 = depth 5.5 and all
depth-5 lots are infeasible, confirmed by sweep) — the same LDK-stack
depth constraint documented on cl_ld and the ds_gr LDK investigation.
Published minimum: 12x10 (buildable 8x6). The gr sibling covers the
shallower depth-5 lots (11x9 up); a sub-depth-6 brief on this topology
routes there automatically via fallback_topology (general infeasibility-
triggered fallback, NOT a fallback_below_buildable_sqm area gate — the
binding constraint is DEPTH, not area, so a wide-but-shallow lot must
reach the solve attempt and fail rather than be gated on area).
UPPER-WIDTH LIMIT ~ buildable width 12 (17x12 = buildable 13x8 is
infeasible). This is SOFT, not structural: it's the public-room aspect
cap kicking in as the LDK column widens (loosening the aspect override
makes 17x12 solve). Those sizes are past the 2BR floor-area band anyway,
so the cap is left in place.
claim_dead_strips: true. At 12x10 the layout tiles clean on its own, but
at larger sizes (14x10+) master's floating front-band width leaves small
unclaimed interior pockets (up to ~2.8 m2 at 15x11). The flag runs the
dead-strip claimer on the single-storey realize so living absorbs those
pockets as L-alcoves — 0 dead space across the band, still 0 warnings.
Realized-geometry cleanup only; never affects feasibility.
Carport / wet-stack / mirroring all unchanged from the gr sibling.
